body:
## Problem

`after/lsp/<name>.lua` is documented to merge on top of earlier `lsp/<name>.lua` files in `runtimepath`, not replace them.

However, if `vim.lsp.enable('<name>')` runs before a plugin providing `lsp/<name>.lua` is added to `runtimepath`, the resolved config is cached too early and is not invalidated when `runtimepath` later changes.

As a result, a config `after/lsp/lua_ls.lua` without `cmd` can appear to "overwrite" the plugin config, because the plugin config is never re-merged after it becomes available.

## Steps to reproduce

```
nvim --clean -u minimal.lua
```

```lua
local plugins = {
  ['nvim-lspconfig'] = 'https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig.git',
}

local install_paths = {}

for name, url in pairs(plugins) do
  local install_path = vim.fn.fnamemodify('nvim_issue/' .. name, ':p')
  if vim.fn.isdirectory(install_path) == 0 then
    vim.fn.system { 'git', 'clone', '--depth=1', url, install_path }
  end
  install_paths[name] = install_path
end

local tmp = vim.fn.tempname()
local user_rtp = tmp .. '/user'

vim.fn.mkdir(user_rtp .. '/after/lsp', 'p')
vim.fn.writefile({
  'return {',
  '  settings = {',
  '    Lua = {',
  "      diagnostics = { globals = { 'vim' } },",
  '    },',
  '  },',
  '}',
}, user_rtp .. '/after/lsp/lua_ls.lua')

local function dump(label)
  local cfg = vim.lsp.config.lua_ls or {}
  print(('=== %s ==='):format(label))
  print(vim.inspect {
    runtime_files = vim.api.nvim_get_runtime_file('lsp/lua_ls.lua', true),
    cmd = cfg.cmd,
    filetypes = cfg.filetypes,
    settings = cfg.settings,
  })
end

vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('VimEnter', {
  once = true,
  callback = function()
    vim.o.runtimepath = table.concat({
      user_rtp,
      vim.env.VIMRUNTIME,
      user_rtp .. '/after',
    }, ',')

    print('before enable:')
    print(vim.inspect(vim.api.nvim_get_runtime_file('lsp/lua_ls.lua', true)))

    vim.lsp.enable('lua_ls')
    dump('after enable, before adding nvim-lspconfig')

    vim.o.runtimepath = table.concat({
      user_rtp,
      vim.env.VIMRUNTIME,
      install_paths['nvim-lspconfig'],
      user_rtp .. '/after',
    }, ',')

    dump('after adding nvim-lspconfig to runtimepath')

    vim.lsp.enable('lua_ls', false)
    vim.lsp.enable('lua_ls')

    dump('after disable/re-enable')
    print('run :messages if needed to review the output')
  end,
})

```

## Expected behavior

Configs placed in `after/lsp` properly merge with other configs in runtime path.

## System info

- Nvim version (nvim -v): `v0.12.0-dev-2580 g8c2b6d6062-Homebrew` neovim/neovim@8c2b6d6062
- Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?: not testes
- Operating system/version: Darwin 25.3.0
- Terminal name/version: ghostty 1.3.1
- $TERM environment variable: `xterm-ghostty`
- Installation: ?
title: lsp: configs in `after/lsp` not properly merged with configs in `runtime` path
